svg.title: Valkyrja exception hierarchy — language agnostic
svg.desc: Language agnostic naming convention and inheritance hierarchy for Valkyrja throwables and exceptions, with a language mapping table for PHP, Java, Python, TypeScript, Go, and Ruby.
svg.text: unique name · not shared across components
shared across Http · Cli · Queue etc.
concrete · one per throw site or group
No class hierarchy. Valkyrja errors implement the error interface. Abstract = unexported types.
No typed throws. Abstract classes enforce the hierarchy. Result pattern available as additive layer.
No checked exceptions. Abstract via ABC. ValueError is the closest to InvalidArgumentException.
Identical roots to Java. All exceptions are unchecked by default — no throws declarations needed.
1. Is the subcomponent name shared across components?
2. Does the subcomponent have 2+ exceptions of the same category?
Yes → add abstract *RuntimeError / *InvalidArgumentError · No → extend framework base directly
3. Each throw site needs its own concrete exception?
Always yes → concrete, named for the problem, never throw an abstract
